title: grewia latifolia
intro: Grewia latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved grewia or bael tree, is a medicinal plant native to tropical Africa and Asia, characterized by its broad, elliptical leaves and small, yellowish flowers. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its healing and therapeutic effects. The health benefits of Grewia latifolia include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Grewia latifolia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
